posts.name: Andrzej Novak
posts.text:
How can I debug a text2workspace.py segfault? I am merging a large (I guess) number of categories 108 due to combining the 4 “years” of Run2 and when trying to merge all 4 I get a segfault.

Any combination of 3 of the 4 seems to work fine.
Increasing verbosity with (-v) doesn’t seem to yield any useful info.
I am on a machine with 16GB ram, which doesn’t hit anywhere near the limit, keeping a look with htop. (I thought this was the issue originally but seems not)

Any way to work around this? Is there some internal roo(t/fit) limit on mem?
posts.cooked_html:
How can I debug a text2workspace.py segfault? I am merging a large (I guess) number of categories 108 due to combining the 4 “years” of Run2 and when trying to merge all 4 I get a segfault.

  • Any combination of 3 of the 4 seems to work fine.
  • Increasing verbosity with (-v) doesn’t seem to yield any useful info.
  • I am on a machine with 16GB ram, which doesn’t hit anywhere near the limit, keeping a look with htop. (I thought this was the issue originally but seems not)

Any way to work around this? Is there some internal roo(t/fit) limit on mem?

posts.name: Giacomo Ortona
posts.text:
Hi @anovak ,
Did you try issuing the ulimit -s unlimited command before calling text2workspace?
Cheers,
Giacomo
posts.cooked_html:
